name: Materialize
description: Declarative generation jobs that CREATE new records (plan/vocabulary/61-materialize-spec.md), the write-side sibling of app-rollup's read-side aggregation. materialize_definitions (admin-owned) declares a source + filter + trigger (scheduled/scheduled_fixed/event) + output collection (optionally with children) + idempotency_key + mapping; the daemon's materialize pass (object_daemon.process_materializations, object_materialize.py) generates due output through the target collection's own existing create/update path, with a crash-safe children-first-header-last write order and an integer-cents balance check enforced before any write. Covers three shaped-alike gaps found by the parity audit: recurring journals, depreciation, and template-execution/CreateWork. system_materialize_run is a manual 'Run now' escape hatch (and, in v1, the way event-mode definitions run at all -- see next); system_materialize_seed is the event-mode (CreateWork) dispatch handler, shipped inert (HANDLES == []): the platform deliberately does NOT rewrite an installed object's source at runtime to track the definition set, so automatic on-create dispatch is off in v1 and event-mode degrades to the manual path. The dispatch logic is built and tested, ready for a future deliberate/scheduled HANDLES-sync job. No dedicated authoring page in v1 -- materialize_definitions already gets a generated admin form (docs/schema-forms.md). Target collections are NOT declared here: each is an existing, already-schema'd collection the requesting app already owns.
